message:
perf(ft-kernel-cpu): pairwise summation in norm_l1 / norm_l2 / norm_lp

Companion to commit 69fce3d (pairwise summation in sum / mean
reductions). The norm-tensor helpers used the same naive
`data.iter().map(...).sum()` pattern with O(N · ε) error
accumulation:

  norm_lp p=1:  data.iter().map(|x| x.abs()).sum()
  norm_lp p=2:  data.iter().map(|x| x * x).sum()  (then sqrt)
  norm_lp p=*:  data.iter().map(|x| x.abs().powf(p)).sum()  (then ^(1/p))

Same for the f32 sibling at the four-byte-element symmetric site.

Add a `pairwise_sum_map_f64<F: Fn(f64) -> f64 + Copy>` helper that
applies `f` to each element and pairwise-folds the results without
an intermediate allocation. The Copy bound is satisfied by the
stateless / capture-by-Copy closures we use here (`|x| x.abs()`,
`|x| x * x`, `|x| x.abs().powf(p)`). F32 sibling provided too.

Surface coverage:
  * `norm_tensor_contiguous_f64` p=1 / p=2 / p=other
  * `norm_tensor_contiguous_f32` p=1 / p=2 / p=other

Test:
  * `norm_tensor_contiguous_l2_pairwise_correctness_at_large_n` runs
    a 1M-element norm at p=1, p=2, p=3 and locks the absolute drift
    bound the pairwise path achieves on each (1e-8 / 1e-10 / 1e-10).

Quality gates: 12-crate workspace test sweep — ft-kernel-cpu 306 +
ft-api 1090 + ft-conformance 101 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-nn 150 +
ft-optim 74 + ft-data 103 + ft-serialize 15 + ft-runtime 2 +
ft-core 70 + ft-dispatch 9 + ft-device — all green; clippy + ubs
clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
